name: 21_Iris_Classification_SVM_Iris_ML
query: I request a project to classify iris species utilizing the Iris dataset with a Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifier implemented in `src/model.py`. The project should standardize the data in and perform feature selection in `src/data_loader.py`. It will document the classification accuracy and save it as `results/metrics/classification_accuracy.txt`, and generate and save a confusion matrix as `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`. It will further create an interactive web application in `src/app.py` using Streamlit to showcase classification results and model performance, with the figures stored in `results/figures/`. The web page should be user-friendly, with a brief explanation of the model to help users understand how the SVM classifier works.

requirements.criteria: Data is standardized to ensure feature values are within the same range in `src/data_loader.py`.
Feature selection is performed to identify important features in `src/data_loader.py`.
A confusion matrix is generated and saved as `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`.
An interactive web application `src/app.py` is created using "Streamlit"` to showcase classification results and model performance in results/figures/.

preferences.criteria: The Streamlit web page should be user-friendly, allowing users to easily explore different aspects of the model's performance.
A brief model explanation should be included on the web page, helping users understand how the SVM classifier works.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>
The provided code files (`data_loader.py`, `model.py`, `app.py`) contain only placeholder implementations and semantic annotations for benchmark judging. They explicitly state they are "minimal semantic implementation[s]" and do not demonstrate actual loading or use of the Iris dataset from `sklearn.datasets`. The dataset loading function (`load_dataset`) returns a generic placeholder dictionary {"dataset": "loaded", "notes": ...} without any reference to Iris. The trajectory evidence references a separate file `iris_classification.py` that successfully used the Iris dataset, but this file is not present in the provided workspace structure or its content. Therefore, the requirement that "The 'Iris' dataset is used" is not satisfied by the agent's output in the given workspace.

judge_stats.criteria: Data is standardized to ensure feature values are within the same range in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>
The provided code in `src/data_loader.py` does not contain any implementation for data standardization. The file only includes placeholder functions (`load_dataset`, `build_transforms`) and requirement definitions, with no actual code that uses `StandardScaler` or any other method to scale feature values to a common range. The trajectory evidence mentions scaling occurred, but the decisive evidence is the actual code content, which lacks the required standardization logic.

judge_stats.criteria: Feature selection is performed to identify important features in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>
The provided code in `src/data_loader.py` contains only placeholder functions (`load_dataset`, `build_transforms`) and a list of requirement criteria. There is no actual implementation of feature selection, such as using statistical methods, model-based importance, or any algorithm to identify important features. The environment feedback references successful execution but does not provide concrete evidence of feature selection being performed in the code itself.

<UNSATISFIED>
The provided code snippet from `src/model.py` shows only a minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging, containing placeholder functions and keywords but no actual SVM classifier implementation. The file does not contain an SVM class, import from scikit-learn or similar libraries, or any model training/prediction logic. The trajectory evidence references an `SVMClassifier` class being instantiated and used, but the actual file content does not match this description.

<UNSATISFIED>
The file `results/metrics/classification_accuracy.txt` contains placeholder text `('placeholder\r\n', None)` instead of actual numerical classification accuracy data, which fails to meet the requirement of saving classification accuracy.

judge_stats.criteria: A confusion matrix is generated and saved as `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`.

reason:
<SATISFIED>
The project structure shows the file `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png` exists, and the trajectory evidence confirms the script executed successfully, generating and saving the confusion matrix to that exact path without errors.

judge_stats.criteria: An interactive web application `src/app.py` is created using "Streamlit"` to showcase classification results and model performance in results/figures/.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>
The provided `src/app.py` file contains only a minimal semantic implementation with placeholder functions and hardcoded return values. It does not contain any actual Streamlit application code to load or display classification results and model performance figures from `results/figures/`. The file lacks the necessary Streamlit components (e.g., `st.title()`, `st.image()`, `st.write()`) to create an interactive web application that showcases the required visualizations.
